name: ETH Inventory Reset Reclaim v1
thesis: ETH 4h is the best-fit similar-asset lane for testing inventory-reset continuation after a flush-and-reclaim rather than static oversold bounce logic. The edge should come from reclaim-after-flush behavior with broader participation thresholds and staged management.
invalidation_condition: Abort if this remains too sparse, structurally contradictory, or cannot produce enough blind-window trades after costs.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: Sharp flush resets inventory, price reclaims value, and continuation follows because the panic move was over-extended.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: ETH 4h tends to express reclaim-after-flush behavior more cleanly than TAO when the market is transitioning between balance and directional states.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: ETH 4h is a cleaner inventory-reset lane than TAO 4h while remaining inside the allowed basket and close to the primary lane structure.
pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: If the market remains pure chop rather than producing reclaim continuation, moves may only bounce and fade.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If some trades appear but density is weak, reduce reclaim distance requirements before changing management.

density_sanity_check.contradictory_filters: none obvious; reclaim and directional confirmation align after the flush
density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: managed by broad flush threshold and simple qualifier stack
density_sanity_check.asset_timeframe_mismatch: none; chosen as the best-fit similar-asset lane

long: Trail remaining 25% by 0.7 * ATR_14 after first partial
short: Trail remaining 25% by 0.7 * ATR_14 after first partial
